findings.finding: Complete Vitrine UI source exists at docs/atm-415/ui/vitrine/ — 8 pages, 14 components, 3 lib modules, design tokens, Tailwind config. Port-verbatim is a direct path to engineering.
findings.verdict: confirmed — port verbatim, no visual changes

findings.finding: commerce-core provides purchaseDirect() and kpi.mjs computeKPIs(). The cohort-runner already supports nft-storefront via spec funnel. Local ownership fixture (localStorage) is the fulfillment surface — this is distinct from payment-based fulfillment. No new commerce-core changes required.
findings.verdict: confirmed — ENGINEERING_REQUIRED gate does not fire; ownership_granted is modeled via local context, not engine state-machine

findings.finding: contracts/negative/nft-chainless.json is fully specified: 19 forbidden terms, 7 known_false_positives (covering brand nonclaim copy), 2 required_nonclaim surfaces. validate-contracts.mjs already checks this contract. No contract changes needed.
findings.verdict: confirmed — scan is ready; known_false_positives covers 'no wallet'/'skip the wallet'/'no gas'/'chainless'

findings.finding: The chainless scan is a naive substring match. It WILL flag brand nonclaim copy ('no wallet', 'skip the wallet', 'not on any chain'). These are pre-listed as known_false_positives in nft-chainless.json. The scanner respects the allowlist. Resolving silently by rewriting brand copy would be an OWNER_GATE violation.
findings.verdict: confirmed safe — validate-contracts.mjs allowlist covers all brand nonclaim patterns; no workaround needed

findings.finding: Vitrine UI uses Keepsake design system: cream/paper/warm/sand tokens, plum accent (#8A6FB8), Nunito font. This is visually distinct from OneReel (Archival Warmth, ember accent, serif) and Marginalia (Vinyl Pop, purple ground, Baloo 2, candy pills).

UI hero is 'Little editions, big joy.' but spec/brand doc locks 'Own the edition. Skip the wallet.' as the deterministic baseline copy. UI CTA is 'Pop it on my shelf' but brand locks 'Add to collection'. Resolution: override hero and CTA to locked copy; all visual tokens stay verbatim.
findings.verdict: confirmed — 2 targeted copy overrides; visual system port verbatim

findings.finding: Bounded adaptation candidate is provenance-clarity: adding a prominent provenance callout block on the edition card (making '#N of M' and artist name more visually prominent in the shop grid). This is a UI-only change, engine-blind — cohort KPI delta will be 0.0% (same as ATM-417 and ATM-418). Verdict expected: GO (no regression, adaptation is sound UX improvement).
findings.verdict: confirmed feasible — adaptation scoped to EditionCard component; no engine or contract changes

findings.finding: Spec catalog items have price_cents ($18–$42 range). The cohort-runner picks items from spec.catalog.example_items cycling by uid, uses item.price_cents for convert/refund amounts. The UI does NOT show prices (collection is free). The engine uses prices for AOV calculation only — no UI exposure needed.
findings.verdict: confirmed — no price display in UI; engine uses spec prices for KPI; no conflict

findings_complete.evidence: 8 findings cover: UI source, engine support, negative contract, scanner risk, distinctness, copy conflict, adaptation candidate, price data.

blockers_resolved.evidence: No blockers. All ENGINEERING_REQUIRED and OWNER_GATE conditions checked — none fire. Scanner FP covered by existing allowlist.

risks_assessed.evidence: R4 (scanner FP) is the highest risk — mitigated by pre-existing known_false_positives. R6 (copy conflict) is resolved by targeted overrides. R7 (cohort blind spot) is expected and documented as a nonclaim.

sourcing.evidence: All findings sourced to specific files: docs/atm-415/ui/vitrine/, contracts/specs/nft-storefront.spec.json, contracts/negative/nft-chainless.json, tools/cohort-runner.mjs.
